programs.name: NJ SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program)
programs.description: Monthly food benefits loaded onto an EBT card to buy groceries.
programs.eligibility_notes: Based on household size and gross income (typically up to 130% of federal poverty level).
programs.how_to_apply: Apply online at njhelps.org or visit your county welfare office.

programs.name: WIC (Women, Infants & Children)
programs.description: Nutrition program providing food, health referrals, and breastfeeding support for pregnant women, new mothers, and children under 5.
programs.eligibility_notes: Income up to 185% FPL. Must have a child under 5, be pregnant, or recently gave birth.
programs.how_to_apply: Contact your local WIC agency or call 1-800-328-3838.

programs.name: NJ FamilyCare (Medicaid)
programs.description: Free or low-cost health insurance for eligible NJ residents including children, pregnant women, and families.
programs.eligibility_notes: Children up to 350% FPL. Adults up to 138% FPL. Covers doctor visits, prescriptions, dental, and vision.

programs.name: NJ FamilyCare - CHIP (Children's Health Insurance)
programs.description: Low-cost health coverage specifically for children in families who earn too much for Medicaid but can't afford private insurance.
programs.eligibility_notes: Children up to age 19. Household income up to 350% FPL.
programs.how_to_apply: Apply at njhelps.org or through GetCoveredNJ.gov.

programs.name: NJ Child Care Assistance (CCDF / Child Care Subsidy)
programs.description: Subsidizes childcare costs so parents can work or attend school. Covers licensed childcare centers and home daycares.
programs.eligibility_notes: Working families with children under 13. Income up to 200% FPL.
programs.how_to_apply: Apply through your county social services office or childcarenj.gov.

programs.name: NJ Preschool Education Aid (PEA)
programs.description: Free, high-quality preschool for 3- and 4-year-olds in eligible districts across New Jersey.
programs.eligibility_notes: Must reside in an Abbott or expanded preschool district. No income requirement.
programs.how_to_apply: Contact your local school district's main office.

programs.name: NJ General Assistance (GA)
programs.description: Monthly cash assistance for single adults and childless couples who are not eligible for TANF.
programs.eligibility_notes: NJ resident, not receiving other cash assistance. Very limited income/assets.
programs.how_to_apply: Apply at your county board of social services.

programs.name: NJ WorkFirst (TANF)
programs.description: Cash assistance and employment support for families with children. Includes job training and work supports.
programs.eligibility_notes: Families with children under 18. Very low income. Time-limited program.
programs.how_to_apply: Apply at njhelps.org or county welfare agency.

programs.name: Low Income Home Energy Assistance (LIHEAP)
programs.description: Helps eligible NJ households pay heating and cooling bills and may cover emergency energy needs.
programs.eligibility_notes: Income up to 60% of state median income. Apply during open enrollment periods.
programs.how_to_apply: Apply at njhelps.org or call your county social services.

programs.name: NJ 2-1-1 Helpline
programs.description: Free, 24/7 information and referral service connecting NJ residents to local health and human services.
programs.eligibility_notes: Available to all NJ residents regardless of income or status.
programs.how_to_apply: Dial 2-1-1 or text your zip code to 898-211.

programs.name: Nurse-Family Partnership / Home Visiting Programs
programs.description: Free home visits by trained nurses and social workers for first-time, low-income mothers from pregnancy through age 2.
programs.eligibility_notes: First-time mothers, low income, enrolled before 28 weeks of pregnancy.
programs.how_to_apply: Ask your OB or pediatrician for a referral, or contact your local health department.

programs.name: NJ Domestic Violence Services
programs.description: Emergency shelter, legal assistance, counseling, and safety planning for survivors of domestic violence.
programs.eligibility_notes: Available to all survivors regardless of income, status, or gender.
programs.how_to_apply: Call the NJ Domestic Violence Hotline: 1-800-572-7233 (24/7).
